notes: Skill registry covering shipped B2 lessons. Wave 10.5 (2026-04-27) added the three U01 sibling skills (to-inf after aspirational verbs; both forms with meaning change; both forms with little change) so the bank covers Unit 01 (Infinitive vs -ing) end-to-end. Skills are deliberately narrow: each one is a single rule decision, defensible in one sentence, and supported by ≥10 items of evidence in the shipped bank. Do not extend by adding broad vague skills — extend by adding the skill the next shipped lesson actually teaches.

skills.title: Verb + -ing after gerund-taking verbs
skills.description: After enjoy, avoid, suggest, mind, keep, finish (and consider in this cluster), the next verb takes the -ing form, not to + infinitive.

skills.title: Verb + to + infinitive after aspirational verbs
skills.description: After decide, hope, plan, want, agree, refuse and learn, the next verb takes to + infinitive, not the -ing form. The -ing form is the red-flag carryover error from the gerund-verb rule.

skills.title: Verbs with a change in meaning: -ing vs to + infinitive
skills.description: After remember, forget, stop, try, regret and go on, both forms are grammatically possible, but each carries a different meaning. The trigger verb does not decide the form; the meaning context does.

skills.title: Verbs with both forms and little or no change in meaning
skills.description: After like, love, hate, prefer, start and begin, both -ing and to + infinitive are accepted in standard B2 English with little or no change in meaning, except (a) after 'would' (only to + infinitive) and (b) when start/begin is in a continuous tense (only to + infinitive, to avoid double -ing).

skills.title: Present perfect continuous vs simple
skills.description: Choose have/has been + verb-ing when the focus is duration or an ongoing action, and have/has + past participle when the focus is a completed result.
